rules.description: For geographic routing, traffic is routed to endpoints in defined regions. When a region fails, there is no pre-defined failover. Having an endpoint where the Regional Grouping is configured to All (World) for geographic profiles avoids traffic black holing and guarantees service availability.
rules.query: where properties.trafficRoutingMethod =~ "Geographic" | extend endpoints = properties.endpoints | mv-expand endpoint = endpoints | where endpoint.properties.geoMapping !contains "WORLD"
rules.fix: For geographic routing, traffic is routed to endpoints in defined regions. When a region fails, there is no pre-defined failover. Having an endpoint where the Regional Grouping is configured to All (World) for geographic profiles avoids traffic black holing and guarantees service availability.

rules.description: Profiles need more than one endpoint to ensure availability if one of the endpoints fails. We also recommend that endpoints be in different regions.
rules.fix: Review and remediate based on recommendation

rules.description: Monitor status should be online to ensure failover for application workload. If Traffic Manager's health shows degraded, one or more endpoints may also be degraded.
rules.fix: Review and remediate based on recommendation
